mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales indicates rising household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms; higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward Rank IC between 3-month RSAFS growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses more than 50bp above its 6-month moving average, tighter policy raises discount rates and slows risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 6-month moving average by > 0.50pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or have mean returns not below unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI combined with a rising federal funds rate signals inflation-driven tightening pressure; this joint condition compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term CPI acceleration times FEDFUNDS change has a nonnegative coefficient in walk-forward 60-day return regressions for two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above its recent low signals weakening labor income and recession-risk repricing; when UNRATE crosses 0.3pp above its 12-month minimum, S&P 500 returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses > 0.30pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases have median 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than zero or if downside hit rate is below 55% in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects stronger real activity and operating leverage for cyclical earnings; higher 3-month INDPRO growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month INDPRO growth has a negative walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp housing-starts decline signals weakening credit-sensitive demand and future construction-related earnings pressure; when HOUST crosses 10% below its 12-month average, 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by > 10%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show average 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average or negative-return frequency below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market strength and supports consumption without immediate recession repricing; the joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform unconditional 20-day returns by at least 25bp on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly real GDP growth indicates broad demand momentum, but its equity effect is strongest when policy is not tightening; high GDPC1 growth with non-rising FEDFUNDS predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-growth signal loses positive predictive power when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, with 60-day forward returns not exceeding baseline in rolling 3-year windows.
